paragraphs.body: One morning, after my parents left the house for work, I tiptoed my way down to the living room, turned on my Xbox, and started playing video games. Little did I know, my father, who had forgotten something at home, came back and quietly sat behind me. Oblivious to his presence, I kept playing for about fifteen minutes before turning around, only to see him sitting there, smiling. In that moment, I felt a mix of embarrassment and guilt. It was a wake-up call, even if I didn’t fully understand it at the time.

annotations.text: Little did I know, my father, who had forgotten something at home, came back and quietly sat behind me.
annotations.comment: Oh wow! That's harsh :) Great job with the narrative intro!

paragraphs.body: My parents have focused on me and my siblings for almost our entire lives, always prioritizing our well-being so we can have a good life. As a kid, I didn’t understand why they were so strict about time management. I would play video games all morning while they were at work, and as soon as I heard the door, I would rush to my room and pretend to be studying. Sometimes my mom believed me, but I felt guilty every time. Despite knowing better, I repeated the same cycle for weeks. I wanted good grades, but my actions didn’t align with my goals. I understood the problem but didn’t take the steps to fix it.

About two years ago, when I spent hours gaming, I frequently heard my parents say, “Stop wasting your time.” I brushed it off, thinking I was just enjoying myself with friends. But I eventually realized they were right—I wasn’t just wasting my time; I was also draining my energy. That’s when I decided to join my high school tennis team, which turned out to be far more rewarding than sitting in front of a screen. Whenever I felt lazy, my parents encouraged me to get outside and be active. Their push for me to change my habits wasn’t about control—it was about helping me live a healthier and more productive life. For some, playing video games is perfectly fine, and I respect that. However, my parents saw potential in me and wanted me to strive for more. Even now, if I play a game on my phone for a few minutes, my mom repeats the same advice from two years ago.

annotations.context_left: About two years ago, when I spent hours gaming, I frequently heard my parents say,

paragraphs.body: Joining the tennis team was a turning point in my life. It not only taught me discipline and resilience but also helped me build a routine that prioritized health and balance. The shift from being sedentary to staying active had a profound impact on my overall well-being. I learned that dedicating time to productive activities like sports or studying yielded far more satisfaction than temporary pleasures. My parents’ encouragement wasn’t just about keeping me busy; it was about preparing me for the future, instilling values that would help me succeed in life. Today, I’m in college, and I see the results of my parents’ guidance. When I earn good grades, they celebrate my success. When I fall short, they remind me that it’s not the end and encourage me to work harder. Their belief in me has instilled a work ethic that I carry forward, knowing that effort today shapes the results of tomorrow. “As you sow, so shall you reap.” This saying resonates with me as I navigate the challenges of adulthood.

I remember being in 7th grade and preparing for a debate competition. My mom supported me every step of the way, and when the day came, I stood on stage, facing an intimidating crowd. My hard work paid off when I won the Best Debater award, a testament to the truth of my parents’ saying: “Where there is a will, there is a way.” This mindset carried me to another victory the following year. Meanwhile, some of my peers didn’t take their futures seriously. When I asked about their plans, many shrugged, saying they’d take over family businesses. To me, taking over a family business isn’t the same kind of hard work my parents taught me to value.

annotations.text: “Where there is a will, there is a way.”
annotations.comment: I'm interested in the way you repeat these sayings--they make your parents' voices important for the essay and create an interesting structural device that organizes the essay. Neat!

paragraphs.body: There have been moments when I worked hard but didn’t achieve my goals. When I first started learning tennis, I practiced relentlessly, hoping to make the school team. On tryout day, I was confident but didn’t succeed. It felt like all my effort had been wasted. My parents reminded me that setbacks are not failures; they are opportunities to grow. Their unwavering support gave me the strength to keep trying. Without them, I wouldn’t be where I am today. Parents truly are a blessing, and their influence has shaped the person I am becoming. Sometimes, even when you put in your best effort, you don’t see immediate results. This can be discouraging, but it doesn’t mean the effort was wasted. My parents often remind me that timing is everything and that sometimes a greater reward is just around the corner. I’ve come to appreciate this perspective. Life is full of ups and downs, and resilience is key. Each failure has taught me to persevere and has ultimately made me stronger.

The lessons my parents instilled in me aren’t just about academics or sports they extend to every area of life. They’ve taught me the value of consistency, the importance of setting goals, and the satisfaction of achieving them through hard work. Their guidance has also helped me understand the importance of gratitude. Recognizing their sacrifices and the opportunities they’ve provided has motivated me to make the most of what I have. Not everyone has parents who are so invested in their future, and I feel fortunate to have their support. As I sit here now, reflecting on the moment my father caught me red-handed playing video games, I realize how pivotal that memory has been in shaping who I am today. What felt like a small moment of embarrassment back then taught me a lesson about time, priorities, and personal growth. My parents’ constant guidance whether it came in the form of gentle encouragement or stern words showed me the value of effort, resilience, and living with purpose. This journey hasn’t just been about avoiding distractions like video games or achieving good grades; it’s been about learning the deeper meaning of responsibility and determination. Hard work is more than a chore it’s a path to discovering your potential, just as I did through tennis, debate competitions, and my academic pursuits. Perhaps, if my dad hadn’t walked in that morning, I wouldn’t have understood the importance of their sacrifices as clearly as I do now. And so, I ask myself: how can I honor their efforts and the values they instilled in me? The answer is simple to keep striving, learning, and growing. After all, as my parents often remind me, “Where there is a will, there is a way.”

annotations.text: As I sit here now, reflecting on the moment my father caught me red-handed playing video games, I realize how pivotal that memory has been in shaping who I am today.

paragraphs.body: You sit with the Xbox controller, knowing the knock on the door will come, knowing the guilt will follow. Outside, the sun blazes down on the tennis court. “Get out there. Burn off your energy.” The voice is loud, insistent, pushing you toward something bigger. Their vision of you is vast, unfolding in ways you don’t yet see. Their dreams speak louder than your laziness. The lessons of this house shape the rhythm of your days.

annotations.text: The lessons of this house shape the rhythm of your days.

annotations.comment: Good work with this poem! It speaks to the big ideas at the heart of this essay :)
annotations.context_left: “Get out there. Burn off your energy.”
